text.title: Scope Change vs Scope Creep Detection Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Classifier still cites an old baseline version after a change-control amendment.
antipattern.root-cause: Baseline_id mismatch with current signed version; classifier verdicts diverge from project reality.
antipattern.fix: Pin baseline_version in classifier config; reject runs where baseline.lock hash differs from current.

antipattern.symptom: Classifier flags legitimate in-scope requests as creep; requester relationship damaged.
antipattern.root-cause: Citations don't resolve in baseline OR baseline is too narrow.
antipattern.fix: Show citations to requester; treat override rate >20% as retraining trigger.

antipattern.symptom: Classifier emits citations that do not exist in the baseline document.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM-grounding failure; hallucinated section IDs erode trust.
antipattern.fix: Validate every citation resolves to actual baseline line; reject decisions with unresolved citations.

antipattern.symptom: Override rate not measured; classifier silently mis-calibrated for months.
antipattern.fix: Weekly dashboard shows override rate per class; >20% triggers retrain.
